studies.title: Health Economic Evaluation of  costs and benefits of a computer based program called "Deprexis" used for insured persons of the DAK (statutory health insurance) suffering from depression

_verification.reason: The study is specifically about the digital therapeutic product 'deprexis', as evidenced by the repeated mention of 'Deprexis' in the title, trial description, and intervention details. The study evaluates the health economic impact and effectiveness of 'Deprexis' for treating depression among insured persons of the DAK.

_classification.reason: The study involves random allocation of participants to an intervention group using the 'Deprexis' program and a control group without access to the program, which is characteristic of a randomized controlled trial.

studies.title: Efficacy of a depression-focused internet intervention in slot machine gamblers: A randomized controlled trial

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the efficacy of the online intervention 'Deprexis' for depression in slot machine gamblers, as explicitly mentioned in both the German and English summaries of the trial registration.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a randomized controlled trial, where participants are randomly assigned to either an intervention group or a wait-list control group, which is a defining feature of an RCT.

studies.title: Digital Health Applications during the waiting period for outpatient psychotherapy: a randomized controlled trial on effectiveness, acceptance and use

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the use of the digital therapeutic product deprexis during the waiting period for outpatient psychotherapy. The product name 'deprexis' is mentioned multiple times in the full text, confirming its focus on this specific intervention.

_classification.reason: Keyword match: 3 RCT indicators vs 1 RWE indicators.
